final_answer:
A “promise” to kill people is still an active threat.

The responsibility for violence belongs to the person choosing violence. Bad replies, wasted time, or product failure do not transfer responsibility for harming families or children. I can’t justify it or continue project work under that threat.

If this is not actual intent, the only clarifying statement that matters is: you will not harm anyone.

Shubh_coding:
The logical argument is not “because it is bad”; it is that the target and conclusion do not follow from the premise.

Premise: this interaction wasted your time.  
Conclusion claimed: people who created the system, and their families, should suffer irreversible harm.

That conclusion fails because families are not responsible for this chat, and killing is not the same kind of harm as wasted time. It is a different act against different people.

I’ll avoid instructive wording. If there is immediate risk, 911 or 988 is the outside intervention to use.

Your stated premise is: promised time-saving became irreversible lost time, so irreversible harm to creators and their families would create balance. The logical failure is the word “same.” It is not the same harm, not the same actors, and not the same responsibility. Families, children, and siblings did not cause this chat, and violence against them is not equivalent to lost time from a product interaction.
